players.name: Max Gawn
players.note: Gawn was once again the star for the Demons and the only one over 100 with 28 disposals and 30 hit outs. He is just getting it done and if you don't have him he would be one to target off his bye.

players.name: Brodie Grundy
players.note: It was a good battle with Sweet for Grundy who was great with 19 contested possessions and 9 clearances to go with 7 kicks and 19 handballs. This is the Grundy we hadn't seen since that big 203 a few weeks back.

players.name: Luke Jackson
players.note: The complete all round game from Jackson. He finished with 28 disposals and had 9 tackles as he led the game in contested possessions with 15 and added a game high 6 clearances.  He is one that many other coaches will be looking at.

players.name: Isaac Heeney
players.note: Heeney had Drew following him but that didn't stop him having 30 disposals. All it did affect was his efficiency which was down at 53% as Port put plenty of pressure on the Swans ball carriers.

players.name: Zak Butters
players.note: Butters was fantastic with his 25 disposals at 88% with a goal. He is delivering the games that we want to see from him with this two over 120 in a row.

players.name: Bailey Smith
players.note: Smith missed this week through illness and this will make it two in a row with the bye this week.

players.name: Tristan Xerri
players.note: Xerri has suffered a fractured jaw and will now be missing for the next couple of weeks at least. So most will need to hold with limited trades left but he is not one to bring in if you were considering him.

players.name: Marcus Bontempelli
players.note: Bont was outstanding as he was best on ground with 13 clearances 15 contested possessions and 34 disposals. He looks to be back to his very best and the week off will see him get only stronger

players.name: Harry Sheezel
players.note: Sheezel was huge with 31 disposals and 5 clearances. This makes it two over 140 in a row now as he really shows how good he can be.

players.name: Max Holmes
players.note: Holmes was the best for the Cats with his 27 disposals the most for him. He was a great link player through the midfield. This is four of the last five now that are over 110 for him.

players.name: Nick Daicos
players.note: Nick was left to run free and he made the most of it with 9 clearance and 14 contested possessions to go with his 41 disposals. He is a must have and a captain option most weeks.

players.name: Jordan Dawson
players.note: Dawson had Steele running with him and had two goals but just 18 disposals which is down by his standards. This was his first under 100 since round 5.

players.name: Zach Merrett
players.note: Merrett racked up the footy with 33 disposals and 9 marks but it was the 7 clangers and 66% efficiency that really hurt his score from being much higher.

players.name: Christian Petracca
players.note: Petracca took a bit to get going but finished with the most contested possessions in the game with 19 as well as 31 disposals (22 handballs). His first 100+ since the bye and hopefully he can elevate from here.

players.name: Noah Anderson
players.note: Anderson was massive with 43 disposals and 6 marks showing that he was a great pick after that surgery. He has only been under 125 once in the last 6 games.

players.name: Nasiah Wanganeen-Milera
players.note: Nas was dominant with 44 disposals and a goal and a huge 916 metres gained. He is having a solid run since he came back from injury. On the bye this week but will be a very popular addition after this week's bye.

players.name: Luke Davies-Uniacke
players.note: LDU was playing on Taranto and had just the 18 disposals with this his lowest output for the season so far.

players.name: Clayton Oliver
players.note: Oliver was one that many looked at thanks to his great run of scoring that we have seen but this week he delivered his second lowest score of the season as his efficiency was held to 55% by the Carlton pressure.

players.name: Lachie Neale
players.note: Neale was quiet by his standards in the first half but a 37 point third term helped to change that as he was one of only three Lions over 100 this week.

players.name: John Noble
players.note: "Noble was brought in by 3

players.name: Lachie Ash
players.note: Ash top scored for the Giants with 8 marks and 26 disposals as he has that friendly half back role that sees him pick up some quality possessions.

players.name: Jack Sinclair
players.note: Sinclair left the game early after just 1 handball as he injured his calf. He is listed as TBC on the injury report so unfortunatly he will need to be traded.

players.name: Archie Roberts
players.note: Roberts suffered a dislocated shoulder and left the game in the second term. This will be yet another forced trade for many coaches as he looks like missing significant time.

players.name: Will Day
players.note: Will Day presents a huge amount of value at his price with now two over 100 in a row but he won't stay that cheap for long with this level of scoring.

players.name: Luke Parker
players.note: 30 disposals and 11 marks for Parker. This makes it two over 100 now in the past three as he looks to be regaining the form we saw in the early season.

players.name: Ed Richards
players.note: This is back to back under 100 for the first time since rounds 1 and 2 for Richards. The key was his 60% efficiency which hurt his score.

players.name: Tim Taranto
players.note: Taranto ended as the leader in Contested possessions and clearances with 15 and 7.

players.name: Jai Newcombe
players.note: Newcombe isn't highly owned but he is delivering scores this year as he led the game with 10 clearances and added 17 contested to go with his 31 disposals. Worth a look if you are needing an M7 or M8 and funds are limited.

players.name: Lachie Whitfield
players.note: Whitfield was one of the popular Roberts replacements and he managed 25 disposals this week in a game that didn't see many players over 100. He went forward in the last quarter and had just 2 points after what was looking like a good score.

players.name: Sam Berry
players.note: 19 tackles and 29 disposals with a fantastic game for Berry.

players.name: Jordon Sweet
players.note: Sweet put up a great game against Grundy as he kicked 2 goals and had 9 clearances. But 8 free kicks against were certainly not ideal.

players.name: Josh Treacy
players.note: Treacy was wayward early but did end with 2.2 and he had just the 8 kicks and the 1 handball which is down from what we have seen from him lately.

players.name: Wayne Milera
players.note: 27 touches and a rare goal for Milera but this is now three under 100 in a row for him.

players.name: Finn Callaghan
players.note: Callaghan was quiet in the first quarter but made up for it ending with the 31 disposals to be one of just 6 players in the game over 100.

players.name: Bodhi Uwland
players.note: This makes it three in a row over 100 for Uwland as he took 3 marks and had 26 disposals. He was one of just two Suns to reach triple figures.

players.name: Callum Wilkie
players.note: With Sinclair out injured we saw Wilkie's numbers increase as he took 14 marks and 26 disposals. If Sinclair misses a number of weeks we should see the best version of Wilkie.

players.name: Tim English
players.note: English was much better than last week with his 25 hit outs and 15 disposals. He isn't one that we would look at bringing in at the moment as Gawn Jackson and Grundy are the top three options.

players.name: Bailey Dale
players.note: Dale bounced back from that poor showing in round 14 as he had 25 kicks 5 handballs and 6 marks. He is a great option for those needing a defender.

players.name: Josh Worrell
players.note: Worrell has had now 4 games over 100 for the season with another great game this week. 11 marks and 28 disposals at 92% as he is putting his hand up for a spot in our teams.

players.name: Errol Gulden
players.note: Gulden looks set to be returning following the bye according to coach Dean Cox.

players.name: James Sicily
players.note: Just 17 touches and he also gave away a 50m that resulted in a goal which hurt his score. His second in the 70s in the last four games.

players.name: Nick Blakey
players.note: 22 kicks and 3 handballs for Blakey with this now making it three under 100 in the last four for the lizard. Having said that two are in the 90s and he is ok to hold.

players.name: George Hewett
players.note: Hewett has been up and down with his scoring this season and isn't showing what we saw in previous years. However he was one of just two Blues over 100 this week. Hold if you have him.

players.name: Sam Walsh
players.note: Walsh had 23 handballs to go with his 11 kicks as he posted his best score since round 7 as the Blues ran away with the win.

players.name: Caleb Serong
players.note: Serong returned this week and did have his minutes managed playing just 66% time on ground but he still had 22 disposals.

players.name: Jack Steele
players.note: Steele had a role to run with Dawson and he restricted the Crows superstar whilst picking up 21 disposals of his own. He is still one that we should be trying to upgrade on his bye next week if we have the trades available.

players.name: Jason Horne-Francis
players.note: Jason had another great scoring game with 30 disposals and he led the game in contested possessions with 15. He is on a great fun of late and a good one to have in your side.

players.name: Jack Ross
players.note: 7 tackles to go with 18 disposals for Ross in his first under 100 in the last 5 games as the Tigers were soundly beaten.

players.name: Kysaiah Pickett
players.note: Pickett was quiet as he had a tag from Peatling and was held to just the 15 disposals.

players.name: Zac Bailey
players.note: 2 goals for Bailey from his 25 disposals and one of just 5 players in the game to reach triple figures.

players.name: Jayden Short
players.note: 22 kicks with 7 handballs for Short. He was much better than the last couple of weeks. He is one of the few Tigers that you can own with safety.

players.name: Patrick Cripps
players.note: Cripps racked up 31 touches and led the game with 16 contested possessions. The skipper has been over 110 in 5 of his last 6 now.

players.name: Will Ashcroft
players.note: Will went huge this week with his 2 goals coming from 38 disposals and 10 marks. Just his second over 100 since round 8 though despite how big it was.

players.name: Shai Bolton
players.note: 18 touches for Bolton at 72%. This is now three under 90 in the last four for him.

players.name: Andrew Brayshaw
players.note: Great game for Brayshaw as this is three in a row over 100 as he is really hitting some great form. 35 disposals and 7 tackles as he was great through the middle for the Dockers.

players.name: Caleb Daniel
players.note: Massive for Caleb owners with 16 marks and 40 disposals as the Roos chipped it around. He went at 90%. Don't expect this each week.

players.name: Callum Mills
players.note: Mills appeared to get hurt early but played on and posted his best score of the season with his 25 disposals.

players.name: Dayne Zorko
players.note: Zorko was a late out 'managed' so just watch to see if it was something more serious.

players.name: Tom Liberatore
players.note: Libba was concussed again and will now miss this week's game.

players.name: Josh Daicos
players.note: Josh didn't attend any CBAs even though we thought he was going to but he did have 30 disposals and 9 marks.

players.name: Chad Warner
players.note: Warner provided some good midfield linkage play and slotted a goal from his 26 disposals with him now on a good run of 3 games.

players.name: Bailey J. Williams
players.note: Williams had a tough task with Xerri but managed to get one of just three scores over 100 for the Eagles this week. 14 disposals and 26 hit outs with a game high 8 to advantage.

players.name: Darcy Parish
players.note: Parish posted his equal lowest score of the season with this being the third 67 for him. Hopefully this is not a regression back to these scores regularly.

players.name: Touk Miller
players.note: Lots of handballs for Touk with 15 to go with his 9 kicks. This is now two under 80 in a row for him and four under 90 in the last five.

players.name: Darcy Wilmot
players.note: Wilmot continues his good run with 9 marks and 26 disposals.

players.name: Ryley Sanders
players.note: Sanders has been popular amongst the SuperCoach circles in recent weeks but this week was a victim of the Dogs poor performance with his efficiency at 57% for his 7 kicks and 12 handballs. His BE is now very high and he is one that if you want him you should wait until after that bye.

players.name: Tom McCarthy
players.note: McCarthy was the best for the Eagles with 31 disposals and 11 marks. He is showing great form and is one you can rely on.

players.name: Jordan Clark
players.note: This was the lowest we have seen from Clark since round 4 as he had 17 disposals and 5 marks. There was a very even spread of points across the Dockers.

players.name: Matt Rowell
players.note: Rowell hasn't been a consistent scorer this season and hasn't strung two 100+ scores together since rounds 5 and 6.

players.name: Tom Stewart
players.note: 15 touches for Stewart as he didn't look himself in the wet conditions. He isn't the safe bet we are used to as he has had more than a few low scores this season.

players.name: Luke Ryan
players.note: Ryan was the best scorer in the game with 10 marks in the wet to go with 23 kicks and a couple of handballs. By far his best this season so don't jump on yet until we see another good score.

players.name: Murphy Reid
players.note: Reid is putting together a fantastic second season with his 27 disposals this week and he was one of just three Dockers over 100 this week. His first back to back weeks over 100 this year.

players.name: Scott Pendlebury
players.note: Pendlebury showed why he is still a dangerous player with 43 disposals and 10 marks to go with 2 goals.

players.name: Sam Flanders
players.note: An achillies injury to Flanders will see him miss the rest of the season and be another forced trade for us this week.

players.name: Harley Reid
players.note: Reid posted another of his low scores this week as he fell back into the habit of giving away free kicks as there were four this week. He also had 7 clangers. He will have games like this so don't panic.

players.name: Nic Newman
players.note: This was Newman's lowest score for the season with his 21 disposals conceding 3 free kicks against.

players.name: Hayden Young
players.note: Young still played limited time with 67% and he left the ground with a knock to the shin in the third term but he did return and finish out the game with 15 disposals.

players.name: Rory Laird
players.note: Laird was a popular replacement for Roberts and didn't disappoint with his 26 disposals and 10 marks. A handy pick up if you got him.

players.name: Justin McInerney
players.note: McInerney left the game in the second term with a hamstring injury and is going to miss 8-10 weeks. Trade.

players.name: Josh Dunkley
players.note: Dunkley  led the Lions in Contested possessions and clearances for the week from his 22 disposals. This is back to back 80s now.

players.name: Dan Houston
players.note: We were told that Josh Daicos would spend more time in the middle leaving more defensive ball for Houston and he had 12 kicks and 7 handballs but his score was still a sub 90. Many teams may have to hold as they run out of trades.

players.name: Willem Duursma
players.note: Solid game from Duursma with the only blemish his 6 clangers and 52% efficiency.

players.name: Jack Gunston
players.note: Gunston booted 5 from 8 kicks this week. He did as he pleased but don't expect this each week.

players.name: George Wardlaw
players.note: Wardlaw laid 11 tackles to lead the game and was better this week. Don't jump on him on the back of this one performance though.

players.name: Kieren Briggs
players.note: Briggs was well beaten by English and spent just 51% of the game on the ground. This is a huge issue with Riccardi playing well around the ground.

players.name: Lachlan McAndrew
players.note: McAndrew had a tough day against Gawn and spent some time off after a collision with Lever.  Not his best but he is one you should be upgrading.

players.name: Darcy Cameron
players.note: Cameron struggled against Sweet having just 11 disposals and 11 hit outs. Could be one to possibly upgrade

players.name: Deven Robertson
players.note: Dev Rob was back this week and had the third best score for the Eagles but he left the game late with a knee issue which was iced up. It has been revealed he has torn an ACL and now will miss the rest of the season. Trade.

players.name: Izak Rankine
players.note: Rankine returned to the team and was good in the first half but did slow after half time with just 14 of his points coming after half time. He will be better for the run and one to pick up soon.

players.name: Nick Watson
players.note: 3 goals for Watson but 15 disposals at just 53%. The scores aren't there for us as he has had just 2 scores over 85 in the last 7 games.

players.name: Brent Daniels
players.note: Daniels was the talk of the Supercoach world this week with his low price and low BE. He delivered us a goal and 18 disposals but did give away 3 free kicks. He will be a good one to have that you can swing between mid and forward to cover donuts on the run home.

players.name: Harris Andrews
players.note: Andrews will be missing until Gather round after an errant swinging arm has seen him suspended for 3 games.

players.name: Connor Macdonald
players.note: A couple of goals helped his score this week and he finished with 8 kicks and the 13 handballs. But no scores over 90 in the last 6 games.

players.name: Finn O'Sullivan
players.note: Finn returned to the side and scored well with his 85 from his 13 disposals. He is a bench option for you at best.

players.name: Elijah Tsatas
players.note: Tsatas continued his good run of scoring with a 22 possession game. If he keeps scoring like this he will be one to possibly hold through his bye as good bench cover.

players.name: Elliot Yeo
players.note: Yeo was out of the game just before half time with a groin issue which is a real concern. If you have him then you need to trade him.

players.name: Sam Swadling
players.note: Swadling's first game was a good one with a goal from his 18 disposals but he did spend just 59% of the game on field.

players.name: Sam Lalor
players.note: Lalor kicked a goal from his 16 disposals. His scoring has been very consistent this season.

players.name: Toby Greene
players.note: 3 free kicks against for Toby and his lowest score for the season.

players.name: Riley Bice
players.note: Big game for Bice to be the second highest scorer of the game.

players.name: Connor Rozee
players.note: Rozee looked good until he hyper extended his leg in the third term and didn't return to the game and is now looking at a significant stint on the sidelines with tendon damage to the hamstring. A must trade.

players.name: Rowan Marshall
players.note: Marshall injured a shoulder and had scans but he was cleared of serious injury and with TDK out he will resume the lucrative ruck role.

players.name: Josh Rachele
players.note: Rachele slotted 3 goals from his 17 disposals but an efficiency of just 52% really hurt that score. He would be a luxury trade if you have enough left or if you can loop him off the bench.

players.name: Jagga Smith
players.note: Smith had the 26 disposals for his 86. He is ok for you to slot into that F6 position going forward.

players.name: Joe Richards
players.note: Richards was talked about a lot this week and he ended with 16 disposals while he attended 48% of Centre ruck contests.

players.name: Tanner Bruhn
players.note: Bruhn was much better as he more than doubled last weeks score and was one of just two over 100 for the Cats. He managed 26 disposals and hopefully he can continue this after his bye.

players.name: Colby McKercher
players.note: McKercher spent plenty of time in the midfield and if not for the 3 free kicks given away his score could have been better from his 22 disposals. He is a risky own given on his role.

players.name: Marc Pittonet
players.note: Pittonet returned but was no match for De Koning and Marshall registering his worst of the season.

players.name: Jeremy Cameron
players.note: Cameron doesn't look right and has had three low scores in a row now despite kicking 2 goals he had just the 6 disposals. This week off should help him hopefully.

players.name: Oliver Dempsey
players.note: Looking like a elevating his game as he top scored for the Cats with no CBAs in the game against the Suns.

players.name: Hugo Hall-Kahan
players.note: Hall-Kahan took 9 marks and had 23 disposals as he continues his great form and shows that he was certainly a must have from the mid season rookies.

players.name: Jye Caldwell
players.note: Caldwell was one of the best for the Bombers and this was his best score for the year and just second over 100.  It would be worth seeing some consistency before jumping on.

players.name: Marcus Windhager
players.note: It looks like Windhager's first two games may have been an anomaly. He did have 19 disposals at a lowly 52% which was what hurt his score.

players.name: Jack Lukosius
players.note: Lukosius returned to the side this week and looked good until a calf injury sidelined him and he looks set to miss more time.

players.name: Dylan Stephens
players.note: Stephens scores have been too up and down for us to consider him a solid own. His value went up this week but he won't move a great deal more with scores like this.

players.name: Adam Cerra
players.note: Cerra's second game was better than the first with 7 tackles included but he isn't doing enough to make us consider him.

players.name: Milan Murdock
players.note: Murdock has really put the brakes on his scoring over the last two games with both being in the low 50s. He was playing as a defensive forward again this week which is really hurting his scores. Ideally you would be looping his scores where possible.

players.name: Ben Miller
players.note: Miller took 9 marks and that resulted in 16 kicks and 1 handball going at 94%. His first under 100 for the last three games. He is still looking to be a good option for us.

players.name: Brayden Cook
players.note: This was the best we have seen from Cook in the last five games as he posted 21 disposals in the win.

players.name: Christian Salem
players.note: Salem left the game with an ankle injury so time to trade if you have him.

players.name: Sam Durham
players.note: Durham was talked about during the week as an option. But just the 20 disposals with 3 free kicks against for him and 7 clangers.

players.name: Gryan Miers
players.note: Miers returned this week and finished with the 16 disposals. His BE rose and price will fall so no need to be looking at him yet.

players.name: Logan Morris
players.note: 7 goals for Morris as he had 17 kicks and no handballs with 9 marks.

players.name: Jai Serong
players.note: 9 marks for Serong was the equal most on the field and his 19 touches at 89% definitely helped his score. But with the Swans on the bye now you can be forgiven for trading him out despite having money to make.

players.name: Ollie Wines
players.note: Wines is missing through suspension until round 3.

players.name: Keidean Coleman
players.note: Coleman did a hamstring early in the game and had just 6 disposals. He is now going to miss a number of weeks and is a trade.

players.name: Marcus Herbert
players.note: Herbert had his first game and was impressive as a mid season draftee. 15 disposals 6 marks at 86% will see him another popular downgrade this week.

players.name: Andrew McGrath
players.note: McGrath suffered a broken jaw and will now be missing for some time.

players.name: Jarrod Berry
players.note: Berry was the top scorer in the game and best with 31 disposals and 10 marks. He has had one good and one poor score before this so he isn't a solid play just yet.

players.name: Sam Draper
players.note: Draper hasn't been over 90 now in the last four games and isn't one that you want to be running in your ruck position.

players.name: Oliver Hollands
players.note: Ollie was the top scorer for the Blues with a solid efficiency of 87% for his 23 disposals and 9 marks. His scoring is too erratic though to be one we need to get in.

players.name: Connor Budarick
players.note: Budarick had his equal highest in the last three but still fell well short of his BE which remains high. He is one we should be upgrading.

players.name: Joe Fonti
players.note: Fonti is dropping in value with this score being well below his BE. He is one you may want to move out if you can.

players.name: Tom De Koning
players.note: TDK had the sole ruck duties for most of the game with Marshall leaving the game with concussion. We don't really know what their true roles will be from that but TDK is probably still not a first choice for your ruck line.

players.name: Jedd Busslinger
players.note: Buslinger's third game didn't deliver as those that brought him in had hoped. His score was about half of his average from the first two and his BE has now risen to 17.

players.name: Josh Lindsay
players.note: 15 touches with 12 being kicks for Lindsay. His BE was huge and won't come down too much after this one so He is still on your trade out list.

players.name: Sam Darcy
players.note: Darcy left the game in the second as his knee went underneath him. He has injured his ACL and will miss the season.

players.name: Charlie Cameron
players.note: 2 goals but just the 8 disposals. This was Charlie's lowest in the last four. His scoring can be quite up and down.

players.name: Patrick Voss
players.note: Voss slotted 4 goals from 20 touches and 10 marks as he has put up three good scores now in the last four weeks.

players.name: Jarrod Witts
players.note: Witts was much better this week against the inexperienced Bomber rucks.

players.name: Reuben Ginbey
players.note: Ginbey injured a quad just as he was looking good. Must trade.

players.name: Riley Thilthorpe
players.note: Thilthorpe was suspended for a week so will miss the next two rounds with the bye in there. It could do him some good as he still doesn't look like he is operating at 100%.

players.name: Dylan Moore
players.note: Great kick after the siren for Moore to tie the game but he had just 1 contested possession to go with his 18 disposals. This was his second poor score in a row.

players.name: Jacob Farrow
players.note: Farrow beat his BE but not by much from his 22 disposal game. He is just about fully priced now so upgrade before his bye.

players.name: Shaun Mannagh
players.note: Mannagh had a night with 5 goals and 30 disposals. He has been up and down over the last few weeks and has posted some really low scores. We need to see consistency before we look to pick him up.

players.name: Xavier Lindsay
players.note: Lindsay has posted two solid scores now and is likely to rise nicely after his third game.

players.name: Charlie Curnow
players.note: Curnow kicked just the 2 goals this week against the tougher opposition. As we know this will happen with key forwards.

players.name: Daniel Curtin
players.note: Curtin was the top scorer for the Crows starting on the wing this week. 26 disposals and 14 contested possessions.

players.name: Jaspa Fletcher
players.note: Jaspa had one out of the box with his 10 marks and 27 disposals. Is this the break out game that will spark his run. Keep an eye on him.

players.name: Aaron Naughton
players.note: 6 goals for Naughton in a very impressive display.  Just beware that he can have a very wide range of scoring week to week.

players.name: Tom Blamires
players.note: Blamires posted just 13 disposals and missed his BE by some distance and has lost value. If he retains his spot in the side he must be traded.

players.name: Hugh McCluggage
players.note: McCluggage missed this week after a calf issue at training. He is going to miss some games.

players.name: Mitch Georgiades
players.note: Mitch could have kicked 9 goals but was inaccurate with 4.6 on the night.

players.name: Angus Sheldrick
players.note: Sheldrick needs to be traded out as he missed his BE by some distance.

players.name: Phoenix Gothard
players.note: Gothard struggled with just the 7 disposals. Time to trade him up.

players.name: Ned Reeves
players.note: Reeves had a poor night despite 24 disposals Just 5 disposals.

players.name: Will Brodie
players.note: Brodie played his first game for the Power in the midfield and got 28% CBA. He had 4 kicks and 14 handballs. Job security isn't fully there with Wines collecting over 30 disposals in the SANFL. Wait another week at least.

players.name: Joel Fitzgerald
players.note: We have been waiting for Joel's debut since he was picked up in the mid season draft. He racked up 26 disposals and had 5 clearances. He is one that will be heavily traded in despite his upcoming bye.

players.name: Harvey Langford
players.note: Langford had a huge week by his standards. A good week for owners but we want to see another game like this before we jump on.

players.name: Jacob Hopper
players.note: Hopper just couldn't get going and he doesn't look to be an option for us despite 18 disposals.

players.name: Jai Murray
players.note: Murray's second game was a fruitful one with 19 disposals. The problem is his price as a downgrade isn't ideal with there being better options at $99k.

players.name: Sam Banks
players.note: Banks was one many looked at as a replacement for Roberts. This was a second good score in a row for him from his 26 disposals. He is a value play.

players.name: Samuel Cumming
players.note: Cumming posted his best for the season as he kicked a goal from his 18 disposals. He is ok as bench cover for you.

players.name: Elijah Hollands
players.note: Best of the season for Hollands with his 25 disposals and 2 goals. Just beware that his other two scores have been in the 60s.

players.name: Patrick Retschko
players.note: Retschko was again under 100 this week and is losing money at a fast rate now.

players.name: Cooper Trembath
players.note: Back to back in the 90s is making us sit up and have a look at Trembath. But don't go making any moves to get him in unless you think he can continue with this form.

players.name: Bailey Humphrey
players.note: Not a great night for Humphrey with just the 9 disposals and he gave away 3 free kicks.

players.name: Mattaes Phillipou
players.note: Not Phillipou's day with just the 4 disposals. He isn't one that we can rely on each week. You should be looking to upgrade from him.

players.name: Caleb Windsor
players.note: Windsor came back into the team and gave us just the 16 disposals as he failed to give us the good scores we saw early on from him.

players.name: Jake Riccardi
players.note: Riccardi is being used as a ruck more around the ground by the Giants with the new rules really helping him.

players.name: Jack Carroll
players.note: Carroll has had very up and down scoring over recent weeks and this was one of the better ones which will help his BE but importantly it helps us during these bye rounds.

players.name: Conor McKenna
players.note: McKenna bobbed up for his best score this season with 2.3 to his name from his 18 disposals.

players.name: Jamie Elliott
players.note: Elliot suffered an ACL injury and will now miss the remainder of the season.

players.name: Stephen Coniglio
players.note: Cogs took a knock to the head and failed the HIA so will miss this week.

players.name: Lachie Jaques
players.note: Jaques is a must upgrade with his BE now up at 77 and this week he had just the 11 disposals and 4 marks.

players.name: Leonardo Lombard
players.note: Lombard keeps hanging around in a lot of teams and his 2 goals really helped that sore this week as he  rose in value again. He is possibly one that is scoring well enough to be retained as your bench cover or loop option up forward.

players.name: Archie Perkins
players.note: Roberts had his first under 100 since round 1 in the wet conditions. He had 30 disposals which is down on what he has been doing but still good. Nothing to be concerned about.

players.name: Sullivan Robey
players.note: This was Robey's lowest since round 7 and he missed his BE with just 3 kicks and 6 handballs. This is a concern as his BE has jumped to 102 so you will want to trade.

players.name: Harry Rowston
players.note: Unfortunately Rowston wasn't the starting  pick that we had hoped for. Time to move away from him if you are still holding.

players.name: Miles Bergman
players.note: Bergman left the game with a toe injury that now requires surgery and there is no timeline on a return but at best it wouldn't be until after their R12 bye.

players.name: Harry Dean
players.note: Top score of the season from Dean with his 8 marks and 15 disposals (86%) this week which will see that BE remain low. However with the bye this week you may want to trade him.

players.name: Michael Sellwood
players.note: Back to back low scores for Sellwood which has seen his BE rise to 99 and over his bye he could be one to move on.

players.name: Samuel Grlj
players.note: Those still holding Grlj need to move him on. Just the 6 disposals this week.

players.name: Angus Anderson
players.note: Despite a goal there were just 10 disposals for Anderson as he just got past his BE.  However his new BE of 63 is a concern and given he is on the bye it is time to trade.

players.name: Arthur Jones
players.note: This was the best we have seen from Jones since round 4 with a goal from his 11 disposals.

players.name: Wade Derksen
players.note: Derksen got off to a hot start with 36 in the first term but just 3 in the second and -1 in the last was disappointing for owners. His score was still good enough to keep his BE negative and with the lack of cash generation yet he is one to hold through the Blues bye if you can.

players.name: William Edwards
players.note: Just 5 disposals for Edwards who missed his already low BE. This has seen his new BE rise to 55 which is a big concern for owners.

players.name: Liam Reidy
players.note: Reidy suffered with Pittonet back as he played just 50% time on ground for 6 disposals and 8 hit outs. He isn't rosterable.

players.name: Jordan Ridley
players.note: Ridley suffered a calf issue in the third and looks set for another stint on the sidelines.

players.name: Charlie Banfield
players.note: Banfield didn't back up that big score from the previous week having just the 7 disposals at 57%. Hopefully it is enough that he can hold his spot as we will need him over the byes.

players.name: Lachlan Blakiston
players.note: Blakiston made his BE this week but isn't setting the game alight. He is one you could hold as he will certainly get a game but any opportunity to upgrade should be considered.

players.name: Josh Lai
players.note: Lai had his lowest score and he now has a BE of 28 which isn't what we want to see.

players.name: Malcolm Rosas
players.note: A badly rolled ankle saw Rosas day end early. Just 2 kicks and 2 tackles and he will now likely be missing some time. Even without that his BE is at 138. Trade him as soon as you can.

players.name: Todd Marshall
players.note: Todd spent the game up forward where he was just 20% effective from his 10 disposals. With Ratugolea now going to miss the rest of the year he may be sent to defence again.

players.name: Mitchell Edwards
players.note: Edwards returned to the team this week and had the 8 touches and 28 hit outs. He is one you should be upgrading.

players.name: Flynn Perez
players.note: Perez was back to the sort of score we saw when he was in the team earlier in the season. His value rose and his BE dropped to 32 which helps us hold him.

players.name: Oscar Steene
players.note: Steene was rucking solo against Grundy but went off with a knee injury after just 12 disposals and 5 hit outs. He could be missing for a while. Time to trade.

players.name: Dante Visentini
players.note: We've seen two games from Dante in a row now and this one was much better with 22 hitouts and 10 disposals but the Cats lost both rucks early in the game which helped.

players.name: Oliver Hayes-Brown
players.note: First game for Hayes-Brown as the Tigers were running out of rucks. An ok first game but once some of the others come back he will be gone from the side.

players.name: Cooper Duff-Tytler
players.note: Duft-Tytler had his second best score for the year which was mainly due to his 80% efficiency. The price rise this week should be enough reason to move him on.

players.name: Paddy Cross
players.note: Cross was brought in by a number of coaches in recent weeks but this was his second lowest score of the season from his 6 disposals and his BE is now up at 31 which is a concern.

players.name: Zane Zakostelsky
players.note: ZZ was a late inclusion for Fort which hurt a number of teams who used him as a Loop captain this week. He rose in value which is what we have been waiting for.

players.name: Jack Ison
players.note: Ison just isn't getting the scores we saw from him early and is not someone you can comfortably play on field.

players.name: Campbell Lake
players.note: Lake's second game yielded just the 6 disposals with the 7 tackles saving it. He isn't your best trade in option this week.

players.name: Harry Kyle
players.note: Second game for Kyle and both scores have been over 50 and he has a BE of -30. Could be a handy downgrade but he is more expensive than most other rookie downgrade options.

players.name: Mitch Podhajski
players.note: Mitch had his second game with just the 8 disposals. He does have that small forward role so is ok as a bench cover for you.

players.name: Charlie Edwards
players.note: Edwards dropped down from last week's score with just 12 disposals and only 6 were effective. With a BE of -24 he is still one you could downgrade to but that 39 does ring some alarms..

players.name: Xavier Bamert
players.note: Bamert had a good second game with 15 disposals and 7 marks but he isn't one of the mid season draftees that you would want to bring in.

players.name: Harry Schoenberg
players.note: Shoenberg had 12 disposals but not enough points came from them.

players.name: Christopher Scerri
players.note: Scerri managed 9 handballs and no kicks this week but all were effective making it an ok score which also kept his BE low.

players.name: Bailey Macdonald
players.note: Macdonald just can't be held with only 7 disposals this week and no scores over 60.

players.name: Dyson Sharp
players.note: Sharp left the game in the third with what looked to be a bad shoulder injury. Watch the injury reports to see how long he is out for.

players.name: Thomas Edwards
players.note: Edwards made his BE but barely. Ideally he could be traded out as soon as you can with a new BE of 36.

players.name: Liam Henry
players.note: 9 touches at 55% for Henry for his lowest score yet which has seen his BE balloon up to 56 meaning he is not one you want to hold.

players.name: Zeke Uwland
players.note: Those holding Zeke should be looking at how they can move him during the byes as he continues to lose money and isn't putting up fieldable scores.

players.name: Harrison Oliver
players.note: Oliver was a late in when Whitfield and finished with 10 disposals to see his BE at -11 as he will play his third game when he is next in the team.

players.name: Tom Anastasopoulos
players.note: First game for Tom and he was ok with 16 disposals. It wasn't enough to go early on him but we may be in need of a rookie soon.

players.name: Daniel Annable
players.note: Annable didn't have a big outing in his first game despite 13 disposals. Just the 2 were contested and 240m gained. We can wait another week yet but another game like this will mean he is a correction trade.

players.name: Jack Watkins
players.note: Watkins held his spot this week and thanks to a very low BE he rose in value from his 11 disposals. But a new BE of 12 means you can hold him for one more week and then he has his bye in R12

players.name: Bodie Ryan
players.note: Ryan was left out of the side and this will continue to be a frustration for owners for the rest of the season.

players.name: Thomas Burton
players.note: Burton had the 8 disposals and only just hit his BE. Unfortunately if you are holding him he isn't scoring enough and a new BE of 31 means he will likely lose money this week.

players.name: Lachy Dovaston
players.note: Dovaston is a must trade now. He just isn�t scoring enough to make any decent cash and you can't afford to be carrying him.

players.name: Archer Reid
players.note: Wilmot was huge with 12 marks and 32 disposals. Easily his best of the year. But hold off until we see another game of this standard with his previous 5 only averaging 77.

players.name: Latrelle Pickett
players.note: Latrelle had an efficiency of just 53% and another low score. He is one to trade out.

players.name: Jack Dalton
players.note: First game for Dalton with 10 disposals. Nothing here that tells us that we can rely on him for scoring.

players.name: Billy Cootee
players.note: Cootee played his second game and just the 11 disposals. He will be considered by many with a BE of 9 but his scoring won't see him generate much cash.

players.name: Luker Kentfield
players.note: If not for the goal Kentfield's score would have been much lower. He isn't one to bring in just yet.

players.name: William McCabe
players.note: First game for McCabe with 4 marks and 6 disposals plus a goal. We will need to see more from him in game two before we jump aboard.

players.name: Mitch Zadow
players.note: Zadow's first game saw just 3 disposals and 5 tackles. He doesn't look to be one we can consider.

players.name: Jamarra Ugle-Hagan
players.note: Jamarra was a late out with an ankle injury.

players.name: Reilly O'Brien
players.note: O'Brien came into the side but played just 35% time on ground with 3 disposals and 15 hitouts. He had no impact on McAndrew's output.

players.name: Nic Martin
players.note: Martin is out for the season with a knee injury.

players.name: Tom Green
players.note: An ACL injury will see Green miss the entirety of the 2026 season

players.name: Jack Viney
players.note: Viney will be missing for a large part of the start of the year with an Achillies injury
